Off Calendar Jul-26-2023 Continued To Nov-13-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance: James Reed appearing via zoom for Ralph Oroquita Jr. (petitioner).
Anthony McGurk appearing via zoom (pro/per) (trustee/respondent).

Mr. McGurk stated that he is seeking counsel. Mr. Reed stated that he's ready for trial and it will take about 2 to 3 days. Petition to determine validity of trust amendment and damage & attorney fees is set for trial in the Department of the Presiding Judge (206) on November 13, 2023 at 9:30 a.m., (order signed in open court).

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jul-26-2023 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-31-2023 Text Ruling

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment And Damage & Attorney Fees
Off Calendar May-31-2023 Continued To Jul-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Reed appeared by zoom for Richard Oroquita, Jr. (petitioner).
Jonathan Nightingale (former counsel for respondent Anthony McGurk) appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Anthony McGurk (trustee/respondent) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

Mr. Nightingale addressed the court's concerns regarding failure to comply with the case management order and provide/participate in the joint case management statements stating that there was a scheduling problem with the March 1st hearing, that with regard to the April 26th hearing from March and April he came to the determination that he really can no longer be involved in the case for several reasons, that the work was ceased and that he probably should have never been litigation counsel in this case. Mr. Nightingale also stated that he is a potential witness to several events, that he joined a new firm, that the attorneys there came to the conclusion that he should be representing Anthony McGurk and that he gave Mr. McGurk all of his notes. The court stated that what Mr. Nightingale described does not appear to excuse him and read Judge Quinn's order regarding set deadlines. Mr. Reed stated that at the time the joint case management statement was due was the approximate time he learned that Mr. Nightingale was no longer representing Mr. McGurk, that it took him and Mr. McGurk a day or two to agree on the joint case management statement and that he did everything possible to comply. The court orders sanctions in the amount $250.00 against Attorney Jonathan Nightingale and $250.00 against Mr. Nightingale's firm which are payable within 10 days of the order. Mr. McGurk stated that he is currently looking for and speaking with several attorneys, requested additional time to get proper counsel to help him, stated that he is hoping to be ready for trial and asked that the trial date be continued. Mr. Reed stated that he opposes Mr. McGurk's request, that there is a serious issue about timing in this case, that the main asset is a house in San Francisco, that in spite of there being a Lis Pendens, the house got sold, and that he fought the title company, but the court expunged the Lis Pendens. Mr. Reed also stated that they are afraid that all of the money from the sales proceeds will be gone, or that is already gone. The trial date of July 31, 2023 is continued to August 9, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The case management hearing and the Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Amendment, etc. are continued to July 26, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Continued To Apr-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Reed appeared by zoom for Ralph Oroquita, Jr. (petitioner).
Jonathan Nightingale appeared by zoom for Anthony McGurk (respondent).

The court questions why it did not receive a joint case management statement. Mr. Nightingale stated that Mr. Reed sent it to him, and that it is his (Nightingale) fault because he wrote down a hearing date of March 10, 2023. The court noted that there is a hearing date at the end of July, and reminded counsel that it puts a lot of time into these case management orders and the need for them to comply with the things they are required to do. Mr. Reed stated that they are on track, that they are prepared to go to trial on July 31st, that Mr. Nightingale was going to make some changes, that he reached out to Mr. Nightingale several times, and that after not hearing back from him, he got frustrated so he filed the case management statement himself. Discussion was held regarding the issue of bifurcation and the possibility of a trial not being needed if bifurcation is successful. The case management hearing, and the Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Amendment, etc. are continued to April 26, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow the parties to comply with the case management order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment And Damage & Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: James E. Reed, Esq. for Ralph R. Oroquita, Jr. (Petitioner). Jonathan Nightingale, Esq. for Anthony McGurk (Trustee/Respondent).

The Court noted that the case management statement was filed yesterday. Mr. Reed stated that they were to meet and confer, that the meet and confer did not take place because he had emergeency open heart surgery, and that he was unable to work on the joint case management statement until last week. Mr. Reed further stated that he attempted to contact Mr. Nightingale who at that point was out of the office, that he attempted to file the case management statement without Mr. Nightingale's signature, and that it was rejected because Mr. Nigtingale's signature was missing. The Court inquires about settlement efforts. Mr. Reed stated that the parties are far apart and that mediation would not be worth it/would not do any good. The Court stated that the parties being far apart might be the case but counsel should be prepared to expect some pressure on March 1st.

The following future dates were confirmed by the Court:
Case Management Hearing set for March 1, 2023, at 9:00 a.m.
Case Management Hearing set for May 31, 2023, at 9:00 a.m.
Trial date of July 1, 2023, at 9:00 a.m.

The Court referred counsel to pages 3-6 of the Court's 10/31/22 Case Management Order noting that those pages contain very important information about what is needed prior to trial. In addition, the Court stated that a joint case management statement should be filed with the court 10-15 days prior to the March 1st case management hearing.

Judge: Ceclia Castellanos. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Lozado. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Text Ruling

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment And Damage & Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Oct-31-2022 Continued To Nov-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Richard Veres, via Zoom, for Ralph R. Oroquita Jr., Petitioner; Jonathan Nightengale, via Zoom, for Anthony McGurk, Objector.

The court acknowledges Mr. Nightengale's proof of service that is pending in the court's e-filing queue and confirms with counsel that the proof of service is as to his responsive pleading filed Aug-27-2021. The court and counsel discuss the status of this matter noting that pro bono was unsuccessful. Mr. Veres advises that he believes that the matter is ready for trial with Mr. Nightengale agreeing as he reports that the matter has somewhat stalled. The court advises counsel as to its plan to issue a case management order which will set forth case management hearing dates and a trial date, amongst other case management and trial related orders.

The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust, etc., is taken under submission pending the court's issuance of a case management order. The Petition shall be continued to correspond with the same date as the first case management hearing.

Judge: Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Maloney. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Apr-11-2022 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment And Damage & Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Apr-11-2022 Continued To May-16-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Reed, via Zoom, for Ralph R. Oroquita, Petitioner.

Mr. James reports that mediation did not result in any movement in this matter and doesn't believe that additional mediation will be fruitful.

After additional discussion, the Status of Mediation is taken off calendar. The Petition to Determine, etc., is continued to May-16-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Re Lis Pendens
Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Reed appeared by zoom for Ralph Oroquita, Jr. (petitioner).
Jonathan Nightingale appeared by zoom for Anthony McGurk (respondent).
Edward Smith appeared by zoom for Kathy Kuang and Shu Ruan (interested parties).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions in response to the tentative ruling. Upon conclusion of argument, the court confirmed its tentative ruling. Petitioner's Request for Judicial Notice is granted (see tentative ruling for details). The Motion for Reconsideration Re: Expungement of Lis Pendens is denied (see tentative ruling for details).

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: H. Singh. Not reported.

Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Text Ruling

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Amendment And Damage & Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Dec-20-2021 Continued To Apr-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Reed, counsel for Ralph Oroquita Jr., Petitioner (appearing by video conference); Jonathan Nightingale, Esq., counsel for Anthony McGurk, trustee (appearing by video conference), Stacy Quan, Esq, (observing by video conference)

The Court queries counsel as to the status of the petition. Mr. Reed reports that a Motion for Reconsideration is to be heard April 7, 2022 and that a Motion to Compel is scheduled for May 2022. Mr. Reed reports that his client would be amenable to mediation.

Mr. Nightingale reports on the status of the family tree declaration. He also advises that his client may reluctantly participate in mediation but notes settlement may require the participation of the title company and/or its insurer.

After discussion, the Court orders the matter to pro bono mediation to be completed by March 21, 2022. The Court sets hearing on the status of mediation for April 11, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Dillingham. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Counsel provides oral argument in support of their positions. The court confirms that it is adopting its tentative ruling granting motion to intervene and expunge notices of pending action (lis pendens).
